agent_output: {"executive_summary":"Integration architecture for a logistics platform connecting three external systems (SAP ERP via SFTP/XML, DHL Carrier API via REST/webhooks, Analytics via Kafka) through an event-driven backbone. The design uses an Anti-Corruption Layer per external system to isolate the internal domain model from external data formats and protocols, a central event bus (Kafka) as the canonical event backbone, and dedicated adapter services per integration point. This keeps the core domain clean while handling the radically different integration protocols (batch SFTP, synchronous REST/webhooks, streaming) without cross-contamination.","architecture":{"components":[{"name":"Order Domain Service","responsibility":"Owns the lifecycle of orders: placement, confirmation, and all business rules governing order state transitions. Publishes OrderPlaced and OrderConfirmed domain events.","boundaries":"Knows nothing about SAP, DHL, or Kafka directly. Communicates exclusively through domain events and its own API."},{"name":"Shipment Domain Service","responsibility":"Owns shipment lifecycle: creation, delivery attempts, completion, failure. Publishes ShipmentCreated, DeliveryAttempted, DeliveryCompleted, DeliveryFailed domain events.","boundaries":"Knows nothing about external carriers. Receives delivery status updates through an internal port, not directly from DHL."},{"name":"SAP ERP Adapter (System A ACL)","responsibility":"Polls SFTP endpoint every 15 minutes, retrieves XML files, parses and validates XML against known SAP schema, translates confirmed orders into internal OrderConfirmed commands, and writes acknowledgement files back to SFTP. Handles idempotency via file-tracking ledger (filename + hash).","boundaries":"Isolated process. Only dependency is the Order Domain Service command API and the SFTP connection. Owns the XML-to-domain translation. No SAP concepts leak beyond this boundary."},{"name":"DHL Carrier Adapter (System B ACL)","responsibility":"Two sub-components: (1) Outbound — consumes ShipmentCreated events and calls DHL REST API to book shipments, with retry/circuit-breaker. (2) Inbound — exposes a lightweight webhook endpoint that receives DHL status callbacks, validates HMAC signatures, immediately enqueues the raw payload onto an internal queue, and returns 200 within 500ms. A background processor then translates DHL statuses into domain commands (ConfirmDeliveryAttempt, ConfirmDeliveryCompleted, ConfirmDeliveryFailed).","boundaries":"Owns the DHL-specific data model. Webhook endpoint is stateless and fast. Translation logic is fully contained. No DHL carrier concepts leak into the domain."},{"name":"Analytics Event Publisher (System C ACL)","responsibility":"Subscribes to ALL internal domain events from the event bus, transforms them into the analytics platform's expected event schema (potentially enriched with denormalized context), and publishes to the designated external Kafka topic(s) for the analytics platform.","boundaries":"Read-only consumer of domain events. Owns the mapping to the analytics schema. Failure here never impacts order or shipment processing."},{"name":"Internal Event Bus (Kafka)","responsibility":"Central nervous system. All domain events are published here. Serves as the canonical event log, enables replay, and decouples all producers from consumers.","boundaries":"Infrastructure component. Domain services publish to it; adapters and other services consume from it. Topic-per-aggregate-type partitioning strategy."},{"name":"Webhook Ingress Gateway","responsibility":"Thin HTTP layer that receives DHL webhook callbacks, performs TLS termination, rate limiting, HMAC validation, and forwards to the DHL Carrier Adapter's internal queue. Designed to always respond under 500ms.","boundaries":"No business logic. Pure infrastructure concern. Can be a lightweight reverse proxy or a minimal HTTP handler."}],"interfaces":[{"from":"Order Domain Service","to":"Internal Event Bus (Kafka)","contract":"Publishes OrderPlaced{orderId, items, timestamp} and OrderConfirmed{orderId, confirmedAt, sapReference} to topic 'orders'","coupling_type":"event (asynchronous, loose)"},{"from":"Shipment Domain Service","to":"Internal Event Bus (Kafka)","contract":"Publishes ShipmentCreated{shipmentId, orderId, carrier, trackingNumber}, DeliveryAttempted{shipmentId, attemptNumber, timestamp}, DeliveryCompleted{shipmentId, completedAt, signature}, DeliveryFailed{shipmentId, reason, failedAt} to topic 'shipments'","coupling_type":"event (asynchronous, loose)"},{"from":"SAP ERP Adapter","to":"Order Domain Service","contract":"Calls ConfirmOrder command {orderId, sapDocumentNumber, confirmedItems} via synchronous internal API","coupling_type":"command (synchronous, request-response)"},{"from":"Internal Event Bus (Kafka)","to":"DHL Carrier Adapter (Outbound)","contract":"Consumes ShipmentCreated events from topic 'shipments' to trigger DHL booking","coupling_type":"event (asynchronous, loose)"},{"from":"DHL Carrier Adapter (Inbound)","to":"Shipment Domain Service","contract":"Calls ConfirmDeliveryStatus command {shipmentId, status, timestamp, rawCarrierPayload} via synchronous internal API","coupling_type":"command (synchronous, request-response)"},{"from":"Webhook Ingress Gateway","to":"DHL Carrier Adapter (Inbound)","contract":"HTTP POST of raw DHL webhook payload to internal queue/endpoint, must complete < 200ms to leave budget for processing","coupling_type":"infrastructure (synchronous HTTP, tightly time-bounded)"},{"from":"Internal Event Bus (Kafka)","to":"Analytics Event Publisher","contract":"Consumes ALL domain events from topics 'orders' and 'shipments'","coupling_type":"event (asynchronous, loose)"},{"from":"Analytics Event Publisher","to":"Analytics Platform (System C)","contract":"Publishes transformed events to external Kafka topic 'logistics.events.v1' in analytics-defined Avro schema","coupling_type":"event (asynchronous, loose, schema-registry governed)"}],"data_flow":"1. SAP drops XML on SFTP → SAP Adapter polls every 15m → parses XML → issues ConfirmOrder command → Order Domain Service validates & transitions state → publishes OrderConfirmed to Kafka. 2. When a shipment is created (from order fulfillment logic), Shipment Domain Service publishes ShipmentCreated → DHL Outbound Adapter consumes event → calls DHL REST API to book → stores tracking number. 3. DHL sends webhook to Ingress Gateway → Gateway validates & enqueues → DHL Inbound Adapter translates status → issues domain command → Shipment Domain Service transitions state → publishes DeliveryAttempted/Completed/Failed to Kafka. 4. Analytics Publisher continuously consumes all domain events from Kafka → transforms to analytics schema → publishes to external analytics Kafka topic. All paths converge on the internal Kafka bus, giving a single ordered event log.","patterns_used":[{"pattern":"Anti-Corruption Layer (ACL)","rationale":"Each external system has a radically different protocol (SFTP/XML, REST/webhooks, Kafka) and data model. ACLs prevent external concepts from polluting the domain model. SAP's XML structure, DHL's status codes, and the analytics schema all stay contained in their respective adapters."},{"pattern":"Ports and Adapters (Hexagonal Architecture)","rationale":"The domain services define ports (command APIs, event publishers). Each external integration is an adapter plugged into a port. This makes it trivial to swap carriers (e.g., DHL → FedEx) without touching domain logic."},{"pattern":"Event-Driven Architecture with Event Sourcing Readiness","rationale":"Kafka as the central event bus decouples all components, enables replay for debugging and reprocessing, supports adding new consumers without modifying producers, and naturally feeds the analytics real-time stream requirement."},{"pattern":"CQRS-lite (Command separation at adapter boundary)","rationale":"Inbound adapters translate external signals into commands; domain services process commands and emit events. This separates the write path (commands) from the read/notification path (events) at the integration boundary."},{"pattern":"Circuit Breaker + Retry with Backoff","rationale":"DHL REST API is an external dependency with unpredictable availability. Circuit breaker prevents cascade failures; retries with exponential backoff handle transient errors without overwhelming the carrier API."},{"pattern":"Transactional Outbox","rationale":"Domain services write state changes and outbox events in a single transaction, then a relay publishes to Kafka. This guarantees at-least-once event delivery without distributed transactions, critical for the OrderConfirmed → Kafka → DHL booking flow."},{"pattern":"Idempotent Consumer","rationale":"Every adapter must handle duplicate deliveries (Kafka at-least-once, SFTP re-polling, DHL webhook retries). Each consumer tracks processed message IDs to ensure exactly-once processing semantics at the application level."}]},"domain_model":{"bounded_contexts":[{"name":"Order Management","classification":"core","aggregates":["Order (root: orderId, states: Placed→Confirmed→Fulfilled→Cancelled, invariants: items must have positive quantities, confirmation requires valid SAP reference)"]},{"name":"Shipment & Delivery","classification":"core","aggregates":["Shipment (root: shipmentId, states: Created→InTransit→Delivered→Failed, invariants: max 3 delivery attempts before escalation, must reference a confirmed order)"]},{"name":"ERP Integration","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["SFTPFileLedger (root: fileName+hash, tracks processed files for idempotency, owns retry/error state per file)"]},{"name":"Carrier Integration","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["CarrierBooking (root: bookingId, tracks outbound booking state, DHL-specific reference data, webhook correlation IDs)"]},{"name":"Analytics Integration","classification":"generic","aggregates":["EventProjection (stateless transformer, no persistent aggregates — pure event mapping)"]}],"context_map":[{"upstream":"Order Management","downstream":"ERP Integration","relationship":"Customer-Supplier. Order Management defines the OrderConfirmed command contract. ERP Integration adapter conforms to it. Order Management is upstream and dictates the model."},{"upstream":"Shipment & Delivery","downstream":"Carrier Integration","relationship":"Customer-Supplier with ACL. Shipment context defines what a delivery status means. Carrier Integration translates DHL-specific statuses (e.g., 'DLVRD', 'NTDEL') into domain-meaningful commands. ACL prevents DHL vocabulary from leaking upstream."},{"upstream":"Order Management","downstream":"Analytics Integration","relationship":"Conformist. Analytics consumes domain events as-is and transforms to its own schema. No influence on the upstream model."},{"upstream":"Shipment & Delivery","downstream":"Analytics Integration","relationship":"Conformist. Same pattern — analytics subscribes and transforms without upstream influence."}]},"trade_offs":[{"decision":"Kafka as the single internal event bus rather than separate message brokers per integration","gains":"Unified event log, replay capability, natural fit for analytics streaming requirement, single operational footprint for messaging, enables event sourcing later","sacrifices":"Kafka operational complexity (ZooKeeper/KRaft, partition management, retention policies), slight overkill if only 3 integrations, higher minimum infrastructure cost","alternatives_considered":["RabbitMQ for internal messaging + separate Kafka only for analytics (simpler per-component but two messaging systems to operate)","Direct service-to-service calls with a separate CDC pipeline to Kafka (lower latency for commands but tighter coupling and CDC complexity)"]},{"decision":"Synchronous command calls from adapters into domain services (rather than all-async)","gains":"Immediate validation feedback to adapters (e.g., SAP adapter knows if order confirmation failed), simpler error handling, transactional consistency within the domain service","sacrifices":"Tighter coupling between adapter and domain service at the command boundary, adapter must handle domain service unavailability","alternatives_considered":["Fully async command submission via Kafka command topics (more decoupled but harder to surface validation errors, adds latency to error feedback loop)"]},{"decision":"Dedicated webhook ingress gateway separate from the DHL adapter's business logic","gains":"Guarantees < 500ms response by doing zero business logic in the HTTP path, independently scalable, can be a battle-tested reverse proxy","sacrifices":"Additional component to deploy and monitor, slight added latency from the internal queue hop","alternatives_considered":["Handling webhook directly in the DHL adapter service (simpler deployment but risks breaching 500ms SLA if translation logic is slow or DB is under load)"]},{"decision":"Transactional Outbox pattern for domain-to-Kafka event publishing","gains":"Guaranteed at-least-once delivery without distributed transactions, no event loss even if Kafka is temporarily down","sacrifices":"Added complexity of outbox table and relay process, slight publishing latency (relay polling interval), requires idempotent consumers","alternatives_considered":["Direct publish to Kafka after DB commit (simpler but risks event loss if publish fails after commit)","Change Data Capture from DB to Kafka via Debezium (powerful but significant infrastructure addition)"]}],"flexibility_points":[{"area":"Carrier swap (DHL → FedEx, UPS, etc.)","evolution_path":"Build a new carrier adapter implementing the same port interface. The Shipment Domain Service is untouched. Multi-carrier support is achievable by routing ShipmentCreated events to the appropriate adapter based on carrier selection."},{"area":"ERP migration (SAP → Oracle, NetSuite)","evolution_path":"Replace the SAP Adapter with a new adapter for the target ERP. If the new ERP uses API instead of SFTP, only the adapter's transport layer changes. The ConfirmOrder command contract stays stable."},{"area":"New event consumers","evolution_path":"Any new system that needs logistics events simply subscribes to the Kafka topics. Zero changes to existing producers or domain services. This is the core value of the event bus."},{"area":"Analytics schema evolution","evolution_path":"Analytics Publisher owns the transformation. Schema changes in the analytics platform are absorbed entirely within this adapter, using schema registry versioning (Avro forward/backward compatibility)."},{"area":"Batch-to-real-time ERP migration","evolution_path":"If SAP eventually supports real-time API or event push instead of SFTP batch, swap the polling mechanism inside the SAP adapter to an event listener. The domain-facing contract remains identical."}],"rigidity_points":[{"area":"Internal domain event schema","reason":"All consumers depend on the structure of OrderPlaced, OrderConfirmed, ShipmentCreated, DeliveryAttempted, DeliveryCompleted, DeliveryFailed. Schema changes require coordinated consumer updates. Mitigated by schema registry with compatibility rules, but intentionally rigid to enforce a stable ubiquitous language."},{"area":"Kafka as event backbone","reason":"Deeply embedded infrastructure choice. Switching from Kafka to another message bus would require significant rework of all producers and consumers. This is an acceptable rigidity — Kafka is proven at scale for exactly this use case."},{"area":"Command contracts between adapters and domain services","reason":"ConfirmOrder, ConfirmDeliveryStatus — these are the stable interfaces. Changing them requires updating both the adapter and the domain service. Intentionally rigid because they represent core domain operations."}],"risks":[{"risk":"DHL webhook 500ms SLA breach under load","severity":"high","mitigation":"Webhook ingress gateway does no business logic — accepts, validates HMAC, enqueues to internal buffer (Redis list or Kafka topic), returns 200. Business processing happens asynchronously. Load test to verify p99 < 300ms with headroom."},{"risk":"SAP SFTP file processing fails silently (corrupt XML, network issues)","severity":"high","mitigation":"SFTPFileLedger tracks every file with status (pending, processed, failed, skipped). Dead-letter queue for unparseable files. Alerting on: files stuck in pending > 30min, failure rate > threshold, no files received in expected window (heartbeat monitoring)."},{"risk":"Event ordering violations in Kafka causing downstream inconsistencies","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Partition by orderId for order topics and shipmentId for shipment topics. This guarantees ordering within a single order/shipment lifecycle. Cross-aggregate ordering is handled by consumers using event timestamps and idempotency checks."},{"risk":"Transactional outbox relay lag causing stale analytics data","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Tune relay polling interval to 100-500ms. Monitor outbox table depth. If lag exceeds threshold, alert. Can switch to CDC (Debezium) if polling becomes a bottleneck."},{"risk":"DHL REST API unavailability blocking shipment creation","severity":"high","mitigation":"Circuit breaker (open after 5 consecutive failures, half-open after 30s). Failed bookings are retried with exponential backoff from a persistent retry queue. ShipmentCreated events remain in Kafka and can be reprocessed. Alerting on circuit breaker state changes."},{"risk":"Duplicate event processing causing double bookings with DHL","severity":"critical","mitigation":"DHL Outbound Adapter maintains an idempotency key (shipmentId) and checks before booking. DHL's own idempotency mechanisms (if available) serve as a second guard. Inbound webhook deduplication via webhookId/messageId tracking."}],"implementation_guidance":{"recommended_approach":"Phase 1: Stand up Kafka and implement the two core domain services (Order, Shipment) with the transactional outbox pattern. Phase 2: Build the SAP SFTP Adapter — it's the most mechanical (polling, XML parsing) and can be tested with sample XML files. Phase 3: Build the DHL Carrier Adapter — start with outbound booking, then add webhook ingress. Phase 4: Build the Analytics Publisher — simplest adapter since it's a pure event transformer. Each phase is independently deployable and testable.","files_to_create_or_modify":["src/domain/order/OrderAggregate.ts — Order state machine and invariants","src/domain/order/commands/ConfirmOrder.ts — Command handler","src/domain/order/events/OrderPlaced.ts, OrderConfirmed.ts — Event definitions","src/domain/shipment/ShipmentAggregate.ts — Shipment state machine","src/domain/shipment/commands/ConfirmDeliveryStatus.ts — Command handler","src/domain/shipment/events/ShipmentCreated.ts, DeliveryAttempted.ts, DeliveryCompleted.ts, DeliveryFailed.ts","src/infrastructure/kafka/EventBusKafka.ts — Kafka producer/consumer wrappers","src/infrastructure/outbox/OutboxRelay.ts — Polls outbox table, publishes to Kafka","src/adapters/sap/SapSftpAdapter.ts — SFTP polling, XML parsing, file ledger","src/adapters/sap/SapXmlParser.ts — XML-to-domain translation","src/adapters/dhl/DhlOutboundAdapter.ts — Consumes ShipmentCreated, calls DHL API","src/adapters/dhl/DhlWebhookHandler.ts — HTTP handler for webhook ingress","src/adapters/dhl/DhlStatusTranslator.ts — Maps DHL codes to domain statuses","src/adapters/analytics/AnalyticsEventPublisher.ts — Transforms and publishes to external Kafka","src/infrastructure/circuit-breaker/CircuitBreaker.ts — Generic circuit breaker implementation","docker-compose.yml — Kafka, Zookeeper, SFTP mock for local development"],"patterns_to_follow":"Hexagonal architecture: domain at the center with no outward dependencies. All external system knowledge stays in adapters. Use dependency injection for port implementations. Every adapter is independently deployable and testable with contract tests. Events use a canonical envelope: {eventId, eventType, aggregateId, timestamp, version, payload}. All Kafka consumers are idempotent.","pitfalls_to_avoid":["DO NOT put DHL-specific status codes or SAP XML field names in domain services — these belong exclusively in their respective ACL adapters","DO NOT process DHL webhooks synchronously in the HTTP handler — the 500ms budget will be breached under load","DO NOT skip the file ledger for SFTP — without idempotency tracking, reprocessing a file will create duplicate order confirmations","DO NOT use a single Kafka partition for all events — partition by aggregate ID to maintain per-entity ordering while allowing parallelism","DO NOT share a Kafka consumer group between the DHL adapter and Analytics Publisher — they have different processing requirements and failure modes","DO NOT make the Analytics Publisher a critical path dependency — if it fails, orders and shipments must continue processing unaffected"]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":88,"high_confidence_areas":["ACL-per-external-system pattern is unambiguously correct given the three radically different protocols","Kafka as central event bus is well-matched to the analytics streaming requirement and event-driven nature of the domain","Webhook ingress separation is necessary to meet the 500ms DHL SLA","Transactional outbox for guaranteed event delivery is the right pattern here","Domain event definitions map cleanly to the stated business events"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact Kafka topic/partition strategy depends on throughput numbers not provided — the per-aggregate-ID partitioning is a reasonable default but may need tuning","Whether the SAP integration is truly one-directional (SFTP pull only) or also requires push-back of shipment statuses — assumed pull-only based on the prompt","Whether the analytics platform needs exactly-once semantics or can tolerate at-least-once with deduplication"],"assumptions":["SAP ERP integration is inbound-only: we poll SFTP for confirmed orders, we do not push data back to SAP","DHL provides HMAC or similar webhook signature verification","The analytics Kafka cluster is a separate external cluster, not the same as the internal event bus","Order volumes are moderate (thousands/day, not millions) — the architecture scales to higher volumes but specific tuning would differ","The six domain events listed are the complete set for this phase of the platform"]}}
agent_output_excerpt: {"executive_summary":"Integration architecture for a logistics platform connecting three external systems (SAP ERP via SFTP/XML, DHL Carrier API via REST/webhooks, Analytics via Kafka) through an event-driven backbone. The design uses an Anti-Corruption Layer per external system to isolate the internal domain model from external data formats and protocols, a central event bus (Kafka) as the canonical event backbone, and dedicated adapter services per integration point. This keeps the core domain
